title: ParseContextKiwixFallbackFired payload
description: Emitted by the parse_context handler on every call that evaluated the low-confidence kiwix fallback gate (chain parse-context-lean-orienting T8). Fires on BOTH the fire and the suppress branches so T10's measurement has a fire-rate denominator and a suppress-mix breakdown — the suppress mix tells T10 whether the gate is too loose / too tight. The no-evaluation case (handler skipped the gate entirely, e.g. no intent detector ran) does NOT emit.

intent_shape.description: The directive intent on the envelope when the gate evaluated. Read-shape intents (status/list/summarize) appear here paired with suppressed_reason=read_shape_intent.

fired.description: True iff the gate produced at least one ResolvedReference. When false, suppressed_reason names which branch suppressed.

suppressed_reason.description: Empty when fired=true. Otherwise names the gate branch that suppressed: single_exact_present (envelope already has a confident hit), read_shape_intent (intent ∈ {status,list,summarize}), kiwix_bridge_already_fired (anti-amplification — kiwix_bridge resolver already produced candidates), no_qualifying_no_hits (no external_technical / domain_term no-hit refs to orient against).

candidates_returned.description: Number of fallback ResolvedReferences appended to the envelope. Hard-capped at 2 per the noise-budget rule.

kiwix_search_latency_ms.description: Wall-clock cost of the kiwix_search call when the gate fired. Zero on every suppressed branch (no kiwix call made).
